Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Revenue grew 16 percent year-over-year supported by enterprise demand for our cloud platform and continued adoption of productivity offerings across commercial and consumer segments.

Operating expenses moderated year-over-year as the prior period included restructuring charges, while we continued to accelerate artificial intelligence infrastructure investment and expand data center capacity.

Free cash flow declined sequentially due to elevated capital expenditures for data center construction and networking equipment required to expand our global infrastructure footprint.

Copilot adoption across Microsoft 365 and GitHub accelerated meaningfully in the quarter, with enterprise seat counts increasing faster than our initial forecast for artificial intelligence productivity tools.

We reduced our workforce in certain hardware divisions as part of a strategic realignment toward software and cloud-first product categories, incurring severance and facility charges in the period.

Item 3. Other Information
